$comment: Example docs/qa/qa-config.json. The scaffolder writes a minimal stub (issueTracker.type=none). The discovery ceremony in references/issue-trackers.md rewrites this file once the user confirms which tracker to use. Unknown fields are ignored — this schema is forward-compatible.

issueTracker.$comment: type=linear|github|jira|notion|none. Discovery ceremony fills this; never inferred silently.
issueTracker.$comment_syncOnFile: When true, auto pass syncs every newly-filed bug immediately. When false (default), sync happens at BRB time after a human has triaged.
issueTracker.$comment_pull: Bi-directional sync — tracker → markdown. See references/issue-trackers.md.

pull.$comment_createLocalForUntracked: ask|ignore|create — what to do when a tracker bug has no local markdown counterpart. Default ask.

issueTracker.$comment_github: Active only if type=github. Both blocks can co-exist in a mixed-tracker config.

$comment_triage: Pattern-based suggestions inside the BRB. See references/triage-heuristics.md.
$comment_platforms: What the repo under QA actually is. Web app -> see references/browser-playbook.md. iOS / iPadOS app -> see references/ios-simulator-playbook.md. Set ios.enabled=true ONLY when the repo IS an iOS app project (presence of *.xcodeproj, Package.swift with .iOS, Podfile with platform :ios, ios/ directory, etc.). The iOS path is for native iOS app QA, NOT for testing a web app on Mobile Safari.

web.$comment: Web app QA. See references/browser-playbook.md. The skill tests all three device modes; discovery records the primary here so it is not re-decided each pass.
web.$comment_deviceModes: Modes to test, in order. Reference sizes; adjust WIDTHxHEIGHT to the app own breakpoints.
web.$comment_primary: mobile|tablet|desktop - the spec primary target, weighted most heavily.
web.$comment_primarySource: spec|user|inferred|default - how primary was decided, so a later pass knows whether to revisit it.

ios.$comment_devices: Simulator names from `xcrun simctl list devices`. The test plan derives device coverage from here.
ios.$comment_preferred: Companion skill / CLI the agent reaches for first. AXe|baguette|XcodeBuildMCP|ios-build-verify|ios-simulator-skill|idb|serve-sim|swiftui-autotest|xcode-build.
ios.$comment_scheme: Xcode scheme for build + test. Optional; companions can auto-detect.
ios.$comment_minOSVersion: Minimum supported iOS version per Info.plist.
